sections.paragraph: an unused root. Arab. أَرَزَ to contract one-self, to make oneself compact and firm; أَرُوزُ firm, stable; أَرَزُ a tree which has firm roots. Hence (after A. Schultens) many take part. pass. אָרוּז made fast, made firm, Eze 27:24. But almost all the old translators have rendered אֲרוּזִים made of cedar (from אֶרֶז, of the form נָחוּשׁ brazen, Lehrg. 512), and to these I do not hesitate to accede. Hence מֵרֹוז for מֶאֱרֹוז and-
